Crucially, the current diagnoses include **"Other and unspecified hyperlipidemia"**. According to the strict instructions, the presence of this diagnosis in the current record provides strong, explicit evidence of a lipid disorder.

However, the instructions also state: "Your task is to predict whether a diagnosis of 'Disorders of Lipid Metabolism' will actually be coded in the patient's NEXT hospital visit." The demonstration cases and general MIMIC-III statistics suggest a high baseline rate (27.6%).
